messages.subject: [R-jobs] Manager of Customer Insights, Dallas area
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Manager of Customer Insights, Dallas area
messages.body_plain:
Ultra high-end multi-state department store chain looking to hire TWO
highly technical AND analytical folks to provide insight into customer
behavior to support decision making relating to promotions, events and
marketing programs for all brands both in stores and online. Yes, Manager
title with no direct reports (better to think of the role as managing a
set of critical processes).

Your primary daily work will revolve around writing R-based queries based
on core customer ?browse and buy? variables in support of a range of POS,
clickstream, email, etc. initiatives for all of the retail groups? brands.

Although R, Base SAS, and SAS/STAT constitute the core query and analysis
platform, I?m guessing you also know one or more of Hive, Pig, or
Cascading and possibly some of the ?secondary? analysis platforms.

This isn?t ?work in a vacuum? role with minimal human contact; you?ll also
?consult? with the advanced analytic team on deep data mining initiatives
including developing and/or implementing predictive models, segmentation
strategies, and optimization algorithms with a targeted performance result
of increasing revenue.

It should also be clear that having a good hands-on sense of the data
visualization landscape with all its philosophies, strategies and tools is
going to make you more valuable when you?re asked to present the analyses
to higher ups.

So?there are 2 spots all reporting to the same easy-going Director of
Advanced Analytics (whom we identified, engaged, and recruited). Sure wish
we could offer relocation but sadly not?

If interested, let me know...



Steve Levy | Talent Acquisition
Connect | Twitter <http://twitter.com/levyrecruits> | LinkedIn
<http://www.linkedin.com/in/stevenmlevy>
Read | Recruiting Inferno <http://www.recruitinginferno.com/>

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Position available University of Oxford
messages.body_plain:
*Biostatistician
Department of Oncology, University of Oxford
*
Grade 7: ?29,541 - ?36,298 p.a.
An opportunity is available for a Biostatistician to join the 
Bioinformatics Core within the Department of Oncology. You will provide 
biostatistical expertise to the Core and contribute to the design of 
studies, the analysis of data and the development of new analytical methods.

This position will involve various aspects of statistics from 
biostatistics and statistical genetics to the integration of *omics 
data, under the supervision of senior members of the Bioinformatics 
Core: Dr Jean-Baptiste Cazier, Group Leader, and Francesca Buffa, 
Clinical Genomics Lead. It includes provision of design and analysis as 
well as development of novel statistical approaches to solve problems 
specific to the Department of Oncology.

You will have a PhD in Statistics, Biostatistics, Statistical Genetics 
or similar subject, as well as experience in bioinformatics aspects of 
high-throughput sequencing or proteomics technologies. You will also 
have a working knowledge of statistical software packages such as 
R/BioConductor and scripting skills in languages such as Python or Awk.

This is a full-time post and is available until 31 March 2017 in the 
first instance.

All applicants must complete an application form and supporting statement.

*The closing date for applications is **12.00 noon on 10 April 2013.*

For more information and to apply please see
https://www.recruit.ox.ac.uk/pls/hrisliverecruit/erq_search_package.search_form?p_company=10&p_internal_external=E

The vacancy reference ID is 107127.


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Analyst Programmer/Data Warehouse Manager Position
messages.body_plain:
UNIVERSITY OF DUNDEE
COLLEGE OF MEDICINE, DENTISTRY & NURSING
MEDICAL RESAERCH INSTITUTE
DIVISION OF CARDIOVASCULAR & DIABETES MEDICINE


Analyst Programmer/Data Warehouse Manager
Java, C++/ C#, Python, together with experience with an array programming language such as R or Matlab

Grade 7 (?29,249 - ?35,938) REF: MDN0452
Fixed Term for 24 months


To apply and for further details please visit our website:
www.jobs.dundee.ac.uk<http://www.jobs.dundee.ac.uk/>

Closing date:  12 April 2013

The University of Dundee is committed to equal opportunities and welcomes
applications from all sections of the community.


The University of Dundee is a Scottish Registered Charity, No. SC015096

The University of Dundee is a registered Scottish Charity, No: SC015096
